description: Enhanced acoustic insulation requirements for residential spaces on Level 01

airborneInsulation.description: Weighted sound reduction index (Rw) between dwelling units

impactInsulation.description: Weighted impact sound pressure level (L'nw) for floors

facadeInsulation.description: Weighted façade insulation (D2m,nT,w) against external noise

backgroundNoise.description: Maximum background noise level in bedrooms at night

acceptanceCriteria: Airborne sound insulation (between units): Rw ≥ 52 dB
Background noise: ≤ 30 dBA in bedrooms, ≤ 35 dBA in living rooms
qualitativeDescription:
Level 01 residential spaces must achieve Class B acoustic performance:
1. Airborne sound insulation (between units): Rw ≥ 52 dB (exceeds minimum WT 2021 requirement of 50 dB)
2. Impact sound insulation (floors): L'nw ≤ 53 dB (exceeds minimum WT 2021 requirement of 58 dB)
3. Façade sound insulation: D2m,nT,w ≥ 33 dB (appropriate for suburban location)
4. Background noise (HVAC, services): ≤ 30 dBA in bedrooms, ≤ 35 dBA in living rooms
evidenceRequired: Acoustic test reports (pre-completion testing per PN-B-02151-4)
Product technical datasheets for sound-rated assemblies

partitions.description: Double-stud walls or concrete block with acoustic treatment

floors.description: Concrete slab with floating floor finish and resilient layer

windows.description: Double-glazed units with laminated glass and asymmetric cavity

doors.description: Solid core doors with acoustic seals

phases.deliverable: Acoustic design calculations and assembly specifications
Construction details with acoustic seal continuity

regulatoryReferences.description: Requirements for acoustic insulation in buildings

estimatedCostImpact.description: Enhanced acoustic performance adds approximately 5-7% to partition and floor costs compared to code minimum

performanceClass.description: Enhanced acoustic comfort - suitable for high-quality residential

notes:
Class B acoustic performance exceeds minimum Polish building code
requirements (Class C) to provide enhanced residential comfort.

Key improvements over code minimum:
- Airborne insulation: 52 dB vs 50 dB minimum (+2 dB)
- Impact insulation: 53 dB vs 58 dB maximum (-5 dB improvement)

Testing protocol:
- Pre-completion testing on 10% of units (minimum 2 units)
- Any failures require remediation and re-testing
- Final acceptance requires 100% pass rate
